generated_parts.label: Dorsal Control Module x 2
generated_parts.spec: Snap-in dorsal electronics and battery module, tool-less lid, wireless board bay, service label and cable exits

requirements: Snap-in dorsal electronics and battery module
Controller board with standoffs and connector orientation
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme and signal returns route alongside their signals
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g <= 200
Each required evidence feature appears in part_tree/geometry_ops
Adjacent interfaces named with exact neighboring subsystem labels and correct joint parameters
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme and signal returns route alongside their signals
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings

risk.desc: Potential interference between cable exit port and service label holder may affect cable routing.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the placement of the cable exit port and service label holder to ensure proper clearance.

risk.desc: Standoff set may not provide sufficient support for the controller board under dynamic loading conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct a stress analysis and consider adding additional standoffs if necessary.

risk.desc: Toolless lid hinge mechanism may require additional reinforcement to prevent wear over repeated use.
risk.mit: Evaluate the durability of the hinge mechanism and reinforce it if needed.

generated_parts.label: Knuckle Mount Rail x 2
generated_parts.spec: Dorsal knuckle row mounting rail, actuator plug-in pockets, datum pads per finger station, fastener pattern and removal path

requirements: Plug-in flexion-resistance actuator modules at knuckle row
Actuator module, plug-in pocket, knuckle mount, datum pad, fastener pattern, service panel, data cable channel, strain relief, wrist band attachment
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access
Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row
Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance
Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles
